zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Elizabeth de Bourdon於1408年6月16日出生在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾瑟其布雷可時，她的父親Sir Nicholas Bourdon28歲，母親Lady Elizabeth Millicent Beckering x63歲。她和Sir Robert Markham I至少有2個兒子和7個女兒。她去世於1460年在她的家鄉享年52歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bourdon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: nickname from Old French bourdon, a word with several meanings, from any or each of which the surname could have arisen: ‘pilgrim's staff’, denoting a pilgrim; ‘bumblebee’, denoting someone thought to resemble the insect in some way; and ‘bagpipes’, denoting a piper or pipe-maker. It is also found in England, as a surname of Huguenot origin (compare Burden ).
habitational name from (Le) Bourdon, the name of several places in various parts of France.

variants.name: Burden
meanings: (southern England, of Norman origin): from a diminutive of the ancient Germanic personal name Burdo, for which compare Burdett . Burdonus and other derivatives of the Gaulish personal name Burdo (‘mule’) are found in France, where it is rare and the similar French surname may derive instead from Old French bourdon in the sense ‘pilgrim's staff’ (compare Bourdon 1). The personal name, whether of ancient Germanic or Gaulish origin, does occur in medieval England, but rarely.

habitational name from any of various places called Burdon or Burden. Burden in Yorkshire and Great Burdon in County Durham are named with Old English burh ‘stronghold, fortified place’ + dūn ‘hill’; Burdon in Tyne and Wear is named with Old English b̄re ‘byre’ + denu ‘valley’.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Elizabeth de Bourdon was born on 16 June 1408, in Sedgebrook, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Nicholas Bourdon, was 28 and her mother, Lady Elizabeth Millicent Beckering x, was 63. She had at least 2 sons and 7 daughters with Sir Robert Markham I. She died in 1460, in her hometown, at the age of 52.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Elizabeth de Bourdon est née le 16 juin 1408, à Sedgebrook, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Nicholas Bourdon, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Lady Elizabeth Millicent Beckering x, avait 63 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 7 filles avec Sir Robert Markham I. Elle est décédée en 1460, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 52 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Elizabeth de Bourdon am 16. Juni 1408 in Sedgebrook, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Nicholas Bourdon 28 und ihre Mutter Lady Elizabeth Millicent Beckering x 63. Sie hatte mit Sir Robert Markham I mindestens 2 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Sie starb 1460 im Alter von 52 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Elizabeth de Bourdon è nata il il 16 giugno 1408 nel Sedgebrook, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Sir Nicholas Bourdon, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Lady Elizabeth Millicent Beckering x, aveva 63 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 7 figlie con Sir Robert Markham I. Morì nel 1460, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 52 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth de Bourdonが1408年6月16日にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアセジェブルークで生まれた時，父Sir Nicholas Bourdonは28歳，母Lady Elizabeth Millicent Beckering xは63歳であった。彼女はSir Robert Markham Iとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と7人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1460年に故郷で52歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth de Bourdon(이)가 1408년 6월 16일 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 세지브룩에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Nicholas Bourdon(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Elizabeth Millicent Beckering x(은)는 63세였다. 그녀는 Sir Robert Markham I(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1460년 그녀의 고향에서 52세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Elizabeth de Bourdon nasceu em 16 de junho de 1408, em Sedgebrook, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Nicholas Bourdon, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Lady Elizabeth Millicent Beckering x, tinha 63 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 7 filhas com Sir Robert Markham I. Ela faleceu em 1460, em sua cidade natal, com 52 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Elizabeth de Bourdon родилась 16 июня 1408 г. в местности седжебрук, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir Nicholas Bourdon было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Elizabeth Millicent Beckering x, 63. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 7 дочерей от Sir Robert Markham I. Она умерла в 1460 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 52 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Elizabeth de Bourdon nació el 16 de junio de 1408, en Sedgebrook, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Nicholas Bourdon, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Lady Elizabeth Millicent Beckering x, tenía 63 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 7 hijas con Sir Robert Markham I. Murió en 1460, en su pueblo, a la edad de 52 años.
